parsed_review.summary: CP00-441 is a descriptor-only settlement-core implementation/workflow slice (RP14.P06.M03.S03-RP14.P06.M04.S20, 40 units, risk B) that extends the established CP426-CP440 pattern. Independent read-only inspection of all active implementation refs (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, contract JSON, model.test.js, validate script, README) found them fully consistent with the prompt baseline, review-baseline.json, closeout-pack-plan.json, manifest, and command-evidence. Identity verified: risk B, unit_count 40, range RP14.P06.M03.S03-RP14.P06.M04.S20, upstream CP00-440, next CP00-442, next_subphase RP14.P06.M04.S21. Distributions match exactly: deliverable_counts {implementation:15, security_audit:9, ui:8, claude_review:2, test:6} sum to 40, and micro_title_row_counts {Primary Implementation Slice:20, Secondary Workflow Slice:20} sum to 40; per-unit deliverable_type assignments in the baseline reconcile to these totals. M03 correctly completes its 22-row Primary Implementation Slice cycle (S01-S02 covered in CP00-440 plus S03-S22 here) and M04 covers the 20-row Secondary Workflow Slice head (S01-S20), with no duplicate or missing unit IDs. Production-ready posture does not overclaim: manifest and command-evidence declare production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review / implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout; the code computes only production_ready_candidate (coverage.valid && slice.valid), explicitly separated from the final enterprise-trust claim that requires human approval. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are sound: freezeCp441Result and SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP441_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (spread from the CP440->CP426 chain) attest writes_product_state:false, no settlement/origination/allocation/working-credit runtime, evaluates_runtime_permission:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, no cross-tenant access, and the full no-leak guard set; no critical boundary flag is accidentally set true. Authority boundary verified in code (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false), contract, manifest (claude_not_final_approver:true, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true), and review-request (read_only:true, tools Read/Grep/Glob). The prompt authority-boundary field enterprise_trust_claim_closed:true denotes the trust claim being handled outside this review receipt (per review-packet.md), consistent with human_final_approval_required and the pack's no-claim-from-local-validation posture; it is not an over-claim. Hermes H14 patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor-only review-packet/receipt shapes (receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, and production_enterprise_trust_claim; readiness-report shows auth_preflight passed with 1446-byte valid JSON (exit 0, not zero-byte/auth-failure); validation-report confirms summary-only review prohibited for risk B with source_inspection_mode read_tools_used and forbidden_tools_present:false. All listed validation commands resolve to real wired npm scripts and are recorded exit_code 0 (including npm test, npm run validate, rp14:settlement-core:validate, git diff --check). The post-review artifacts referenced by the manifest (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is correct for this pre-adjudication review stage per the hardened_review_sequence (this run is the read_only_claude_run step) and is not a defect. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
